event_text: Bolesław I is crowned first king of Poland.
The Testament of Bolesław III divides Poland among his sons.
Mongol forces defeat Polish forces at the Battle of Legnica.
Władysław I is crowned king, helping reunite the Polish kingdom.
The University of Kraków is founded.
The Union of Krewo links Poland and Lithuania.
Polish-Lithuanian forces defeat the Teutonic Order at Grunwald.
The Union of Lublin creates the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.
The Warsaw Confederation guarantees religious tolerance for nobles in the Commonwealth.
King John III Sobieski helps lift the Ottoman siege of Vienna.
The First Partition of Poland divides territory among Russia, Prussia and Austria.
The Constitution of 3 May is adopted in the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth.
The Third Partition removes Poland from the map as a sovereign state.
The November Uprising challenges Russian rule.
The January Uprising challenges Russian rule in partitioned Poland.
Poland regains independence after the First World War.
Poland defeats Soviet Russia at the Battle of Warsaw.
Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union invade Poland.
The Warsaw Uprising begins against German occupation.
Poland's borders shift west after the Second World War.
Solidarity is founded at the Gdańsk Shipyard.
Round Table talks lead to partly free elections and the end of communist rule.
Poland joins NATO.
Poland joins the European Union.
